description_long: This object is a compact, cylindrical electronic device with a metallic gold finish and rounded ends. The top surface features two prominent black circular elements, likely buttons or sensors, and the word 'RAY' is clearly printed in large letters. One end of the device has two circular ports or lenses, possibly for sensors, cameras, or connectors, set into a recessed black area. The sides are smooth with a small, dark circular detail visible on one side, possibly an indicator or auxiliary port. The overall construction suggests a robust, portable electronic unit, possibly used for measurement, scanning, or communication in urban environments. Its streamlined design and minimal external features indicate a focus on durability and ease of use.
description: A gold, cylindrical electronic device with two black buttons and the word 'RAY' on top, featuring dual ports or lenses at one end. Its smooth, robust design suggests a portable, durable urban electronic tool.
description_view_0: Side view showing the gold body, two black buttons on top, and the 'RAY' label partially visible.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the two black circular ports or lenses, with the 'RAY' label and buttons visible on the top surface.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the gold body, two black buttons, and a small dark circular detail on the side.
description_view_3: View from the opposite end, showing the rounded gold surface, both black buttons, and the full 'RAY' label on top.
